title: [Docs Enhancement]: unclear what the package `podman-docker` is
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Current directions for compatibility with docker-compose lists needing to install `podman-docker`, but there doesn't seem to be any mention of it itself in documentation and the listings in the package repose (e.g. arch repo search), just link back to this repo.

If it's all in flux right now, perhaps just mentioning it here someplace searchable would still be helpful.

Is there any plan to create a debian package for it? Or create directions for setting up on debian without it?

comments.body: A Debian package would be beneficial. Documentation and download links would be useful. I've been searching for podman-docker after watching The Level Up Hour (E35): Podman v3 and docker-compose.

We no longer manage Debian packages, so that's a suggestion for @siretart.

As for the original concern - is this specific to Arch? We don't maintain those packages either, so we can't really help there. If I look up `podman-docker` on Fedora, I get the description `Emulate Docker CLI using podman` which is brief, but accurate. We could perhaps expand that to explain that we will provide a symlink pointing the `docker` command and API socket to Podman.

I believe this request is tracked as https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=984770 -- let me know if I'm missing something here.

Sorry for the delay, but I was planning to work on this after podman v3.2.1 makes it to experimental.

Note that this unfortunately won't make it into debian/bullseye :-(

Since this is not a upstream issue, I am going to close it, follow up in the bugs.debian.org.
Thanks.
FYI, the `podman-docker` is now available in debian/experimental and ubuntu/impish.
